readMoreSmall: Lying in a beautiful setting amid nature, Coringa Sanctuary is a place for nature lovers and wildlife buffs. Located close to Kakinada in the proximity to the breath taking backwaters of the sea, the sanctuary is said to be sprawling across the huge area of 235 sq km.
Shri Bhavanarayana Swamy Temple is considered to be a very important place of worship lying in Sarpavaram nearby Kakinada. The place is dedicated to Lord Vishnu in form of Lord Bhavanarayan.

Pithapuram is a town located around 21 km from Kakinada and renowned as a major pilgrim hub in the country. I fact is said to be the oldest holy center in India and is famous one of the five Madava Kashetras, one of the twelve pilgrim hubs and one of the Asta DataSakhti petas.

Kakinada city offers plethora of shopping options which also includes several malls such as Vaibhav Mall, Spencers Hyper market, Planet Fashion etc. There you can purchase anything from day to day necessity to luxurious products.

Another important temple in the area situated in Samalkota, Bhimesvara Temple is known for its unusual architecture. Dedicated to Lord Shiva, the temple is built in rectangular shape with the idol of the lord placed in the middle.

Uppada Beach is one of the safest and the most mesmerising beaches on the eastern coast of India. Situated in Kakinada town, the beach has an extensive shoreline. It offers a perfect recluse to adventurers, solo travellers and couples as well as families. The beach is also a scenic spot to watch the sunrise and sunset while being a paradise for photographers and nature lovers. Tourists engage them

A place of great religious importance, Draksharamam is a temple dedicated to Lord Shiva and regarded as most holy place just after Banaras. Popularly referred to as Dakshina Kasi, the temple is adorned with four entrance gates presenting four cardinal directions.

A place of several excavated monuments which will make the history lovers pulse racing; Adurru is situated in the Razole Taluk. Several excavated ruins found were taken under by Archeological Survey of India (ASI) IN 1953 which includes Buddhist Stupas, chaityas and Viharas.
